input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#512BD4, Dark=#7E5FF2}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device theme at startup **and** reacts to live changes — no event wiring needed.

---

**C# alternative** — use `SetAppTheme` or `SetAppThemeColor`:

```csharp
// In your page constructor or OnAppearing:
var page = this;

// For Color properties there's a shorthand:
page.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    light: Colors.White,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#1C1C1E"));

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Colors.White);

// For non-color properties, use the generic version:
label.SetAppTheme<string>(Label.TextProperty,
    light: "Light Mode",
    dark:  "Dark Mode");
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reacts live** | Both approaches update automatically when the user toggles system theme |
| **Fallback** | If the theme is `Unspecified`, the `Light` value is used |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme, ignoring the system setting |
| **Resources** | You can also theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside a `ResourceDictionary` / `Style` to avoid repetition |

For larger apps, define themed `Color` resources in `App.xaml` and reference them with `StaticResource`/`DynamicResource` so you have a single place to manage your palette.
